indicator_construction.Definition: Number of boarding schools recorded for the state, using aggregated DOI counts when encoded in the source label and unique rows otherwise.
State had documented federally supported Indian boarding schools (DOI dataset)
indicator_construction.Source_Label: DOI Federal Indian Boarding School Initiative (2022)

indicator_construction.Definition: Maximum observed operating duration in years across listed boarding schools in the state.
Mean operating duration in years across listed boarding schools in the state.
Count of listed boarding schools in the state with a burial-site flag in the source file.

weighting_note: Configured weights are heuristic and not community-informed. When primary_weighting_scheme is 'both', the equal-weight composite is reported as a co-primary specification because at small n it produces a more stable rank ordering.
construct_validity_note: Construct-validity diagnostics in construct_validity_report.csv are descriptive aids only; at small ecological n the underlying statistics have wide sampling variability.
mortality_aggregation: Per (State, Condition), Disparity_Ratio is averaged across years. Mean_Mortality_Disparity_Ratio is then the equal-weighted mean of per-condition values, giving each condition equal weight regardless of year-availability. Per-condition columns are also written (Disparity_Ratio_<Condition>).

missing_persons_metrics.AI_AN_Missing: Primary outcome (absolute count); see MMIR undercounting literature.

boarding_school_note: BoardingSchool_Count uses aggregated counts extracted from DOI-style source labels when available.
ejscreen_note: Environmental variables are state-level and not AI/AN-specific; temporally misaligned with 2020 outcomes.
naming_change_note: The composite was previously named 'Historical Trauma Index'. It has been renamed to 'Structural Exposure Composite' to better reflect what it measures: a summary of observable structural indicators, not lived historical trauma. The legacy column 'Historical_Trauma_Index' is retained as a backward-compatible alias.
